slides.title: Lipstick launches skew more care-led than the older base
body: This is a narrow experiment on recent Ulta lipstick launches, not a full category strategy.
The point is to see what survives after the launch cohort is mechanically defined and manually audited.
slides.footerText: Ulta new-arrivals scrape | audited launch cohort

slides.title: The included launches already show the split inside the cohort
slides.body: Three launches make the proposition visible without asking the reader to infer the story from a chart alone.

tiles.body: Counterexample inside the cohort: matte, full and long-wear, but not overtly care-led.
Comfort and care are explicit in the proposition: moisturizing, non-drying, wearable as lipstick or stain.
Moisture and hydration sit at the center of the product story rather than at the edge of it.

slides.implication: IMPLICATION: The launch signal is not universal, but the care-led direction is still visible enough to matter.

slides.title: What survived vs what failed
slides.body: The value of the exercise is not that every first-pass pattern survives. It is that the surviving signal is inspectable and the false positive is explicit.

comparisonColumns.title: What survived
items: Hydrating or moisturizing language over-indexes in launches.
The products still look recognizably lipstick-shaped rather than category-breaking.

comparisonColumns.title: What failed
items: The result does not support a dramatic new lipstick era.

slides.calloutBody: The system passes a narrow but important test: it can generate a specific product-level read after the cohort is cleaned.
